name: session-start
context_prose: session-start.sh is the SessionStart hook bash script registered in hooks.json (10s timeout). On every Claude Code session start it does two things: synchronously, it prints a single JSON object on stdout containing `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` with the static memory-skills announcement, which Claude Code injects into the agent's context; asynchronously, in a backgrounded-and-disowned subshell, it rebuilds the engram Go binary at `~/.claude/engram/bin/engram` whenever the binary is missing or any `*.go` source under the plugin root is newer than the cached binary mtime. The script deletes the previous binary before rebuilding so the freshly compiled artifact does not inherit `com.apple.provenance` from a Claude-Code-spawned shell (which would cause macOS to SIGKILL it on exec), writes to a `.tmp` path and atomically renames into place, and finally maintains a `~/.local/bin/engram` symlink to the binary. The async portion is fully detached so the synchronous JSON emit returns immediately within the 10s hook budget; build failures are intentionally swallowed (`|| exit 0`) so a transient compile error never blocks a session.
legend_items: **focus** (yellow): the script in scope for this ledger.
**external** (grey): runtime systems the script invokes (Claude Code, jq, go toolchain, filesystem).
**component** (light blue): the engram CLI binary artifact this script maintains.

edges.label: shells out to build hookSpecificOutput JSON

properties.name: Always emits skills announcement
properties.statement: For all SessionStart invocations, the script writes exactly one JSON object on stdout whose `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` field is the static memory-skills announcement listing /prepare, /learn, /recall, and /remember.
properties.notes: Static message; no branching. Claude Code injects this string into the agent context.

properties.name: Hook stdout is well-formed Claude Code JSON
properties.statement: For all SessionStart invocations, the synchronous stdout payload parses as a single JSON object with shape `{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "SessionStart", additionalContext: <string>}}`.
properties.notes: Constructed via `jq -n` so quoting/escaping of the announcement string is handled by jq.

properties.name: Synchronous return inside hook timeout
properties.statement: For all SessionStart invocations, the script returns to Claude Code after emitting the announcement without waiting for the build, so wall-clock duration is dominated by jq and is far below the 10s manifest timeout.
properties.notes: The build subshell is backgrounded with `&` and disowned so the parent shell exits independently.

properties.name: Plugin root resolves with fallback
properties.statement: For all invocations, `PLUGIN_ROOT` is `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` when set; otherwise it is the parent directory of the script (resolved via `dirname "$0"`).
properties.notes: Lets the script run both inside Claude Code (which exports CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT) and standalone for testing.

properties.name: Binary path is fixed under HOME
properties.statement: For all invocations, the engram binary is built and read at `${HOME}/.claude/engram/bin/engram`; no other path is used by this script.
properties.notes: Stable path so other components (skills, hooks) can rely on it.

properties.name: Build triggered when binary missing
properties.statement: For all invocations, if `$ENGRAM_BIN` is not an executable file (`[[ ! -x ]]`), the async subshell sets `NEEDS_BUILD=true` and proceeds to compile.

properties.name: Build triggered on stale Go source
properties.statement: For all invocations where `$ENGRAM_BIN` exists and `$PLUGIN_ROOT` is a directory, the async subshell rebuilds iff `find` discovers at least one `*.go` file under PLUGIN_ROOT with mtime newer than the binary.
properties.notes: `find ... -newer ... -print -quit | grep -q .` short-circuits on the first match. Non-`.go` edits do not trigger a rebuild.

properties.name: Skips rebuild when binary fresh
properties.statement: For all invocations where `$ENGRAM_BIN` is executable and no `*.go` under PLUGIN_ROOT is newer, the async subshell does not invoke `go build` and does not delete the binary.
properties.notes: `NEEDS_BUILD` defaults to false; the rebuild block is gated on it.

properties.name: Pre-build delete avoids macOS SIGKILL
properties.statement: For all rebuild paths, the previous `$ENGRAM_BIN` and `$ENGRAM_BIN.tmp` are removed before `go build` runs, so the new artifact is produced from a clean shell context and does not inherit `com.apple.provenance` from a Claude-Code-spawned ancestor.
properties.notes: Documented inline (lines 30-32). Provenance attribute would otherwise cause macOS to SIGKILL the binary on exec.

properties.name: Atomic binary install
properties.statement: For all successful rebuilds, the binary becomes visible at `$ENGRAM_BIN` only after `go build` writes `$ENGRAM_BIN.tmp` and `mv` renames it into place — no partial binary is ever observable at the canonical path.
properties.notes: Same-filesystem rename under `~/.claude/engram/bin/` is atomic on POSIX.

properties.name: Build failure cannot fail the hook
properties.statement: For all invocations where `go build` exits non-zero, the async subshell returns 0 (`|| exit 0`) and the parent script still exits 0; Claude Code observes a successful SessionStart hook regardless of compile errors.
properties.notes: Combined with backgrounding, this means a broken build never blocks a session — the previous binary (if any) keeps working until the next attempt succeeds.

properties.name: PATH symlink maintained best-effort
properties.statement: For all rebuild paths, the script ensures `~/.local/bin` exists and refreshes `~/.local/bin/engram -> $ENGRAM_BIN`; symlink failures are suppressed (`|| true`) and do not abort the subshell.
properties.notes: `ln -sf` is idempotent. The symlink update runs whether or not a rebuild happened.

properties.name: Strict mode in foreground
properties.statement: For all invocations, the synchronous portion runs under `set -euo pipefail`, so any failure of the jq emit or the variable expansions before backgrounding aborts the script.
properties.notes: Strict-mode applies to the parent shell; the async subshell intentionally suppresses build failures via `|| exit 0`.

properties.name: Always exits zero
properties.statement: For all invocations where the synchronous jq emit succeeds, the script reaches `exit 0` after backgrounding the build, so Claude Code sees a successful hook.
properties.notes: If the synchronous jq emit itself fails, `set -e` aborts before reaching exit 0; that is the only path on which Claude Code sees a non-zero hook.
